evals.prompt: My app crashes with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading length)' but only in production, not locally.
evals.expected_output: Apply Scientific Method: OBSERVE — collect production logs, stack trace, and steps to reproduce. HYPOTHESIZE — the value is undefined in prod but defined in dev (likely env config or async timing). EXPERIMENT — add null check logging, test with production env vars locally. FIX once root cause proven. VERIFY with regression test.

evals.prompt: I've been randomly changing code for 2 hours trying to fix this bug. Nothing works.
evals.expected_output: Stop shotgun debugging. Return to the Scientific Method: (1) OBSERVE — what exactly is failing? Collect logs and stack traces. (2) HYPOTHESIZE — form one specific theory. (3) EXPERIMENT — change one variable at a time to test it. Create a minimal reproduction case. Binary search: comment out half the code to isolate the failing section.

evals.prompt: This function was working yesterday but broke today. How do I find what changed?
evals.expected_output: Apply Diff Diagnosis: check git log to find what changed since it last worked (git diff, git bisect). Look at recent commits, dependency updates, and config changes. Create a minimal reproduction case to isolate the regression. Test against the last known-good commit.

should_not_trigger: Write a React component for displaying user profiles
Design a database schema for an e-commerce site
